target.name: Inertia Enterprises
target.founder: Jeff Lawson, Annie Kritcher, Mike Dunne

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $450M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Inertia Enterprises is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Jeff Lawson, Annie Kritcher, and Mike Dunne that has raised $450M to date. The sector peer universe includes one public company (LNZA) and eight private firms, including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen, though no direct same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables exist with sufficient data density for median pricing.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier from the $450M raised proxy—a standard Series A to next-stage multiple—yielding an implied valuation of $900M. Peer-median triangulation was not applicable; while nine same-sector peers exist in the Climate & Energy universe, none met the threshold of three or more datapoints when filtered by stage proximity or valuation tier, making a narrow peer multiple unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $765M to $1.0B, with a median point of $900M. This reflects single-method derivation and carries material uncertainty around appropriate stage-to-stage multiples in this capital-intensive sector, where comparable financials and exit outcomes remain sparse. Given the reliance on stage mechanics rather than peer-derived multiples, confidence in this range should be tempered pending access to additional same-stage or recent-exit pricing data in climate tech.
